description:
Fix JSON output schema inconsistencies in the CLI plugin list and config show commands. The `opentabs plugin list --json` command returns different JSON schemas depending on whether the server is running (has 'failedPlugins' key) or not (has 'serverRunning' key), making it unreliable for scripting. The `opentabs config show --json --show-secret` command omits MCP client configs that are shown in the non-JSON output, leaving no machine-readable way to get copy-pasteable MCP configurations.

Known LOW issues (not addressed): no config unset command for removing stale tool overrides; update command false positive for local installs.

userStories.title: Normalize plugin list --json schema between online and offline modes
userStories.description: As a developer scripting against `opentabs plugin list --json`, I want a consistent JSON schema regardless of whether the server is running, so that I can parse the output reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Both online and offline modes include 'plugins' and 'failedPlugins' keys in the JSON output
Both modes include a 'serverRunning' boolean key (true when fetched from running server, false when using offline data)
The offline mode sets 'failedPlugins' to an empty array (since failure info is unavailable without the server)
The online mode includes 'serverRunning: true' in the output
Existing fields in both modes are preserved (no data loss)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts. Two locations need changes:
1. Online mode (around line 558-559): `console.log(JSON.stringify({ plugins: pluginDetails, failedPlugins }, null, 2))` — add `serverRunning: true` to the object.
2. Offline mode (around line 637-638): `console.log(JSON.stringify({ plugins: deduped, serverRunning: false }, null, 2))` — add `failedPlugins: []` to the object.
This ensures both outputs have identical keys: { plugins, failedPlugins, serverRunning }.

userStories.title: Include MCP client configs in config show --json --show-secret output
userStories.description: As a user who wants to programmatically extract MCP client configuration, I want `opentabs config show --json --show-secret` to include the MCP client config snippets, so that I can pipe the output to automation tools.
acceptanceCriteria: When --json and --show-secret are both passed, the JSON output includes an 'mcpClients' key with an object mapping client names to their config snippets
Each client entry includes the client name, config file path, and the JSON config object (matching what printMcpClientConfigs would display)
When --json is passed without --show-secret, the 'mcpClients' key is NOT included (secret would be required)
When --json is passed without --show-secret, behavior is unchanged (just config + masked secret)
The non-JSON output of --show-secret remains unchanged
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
